name: Digital Twin Explorer
description: The /digital-twin completeness matrix + the cell-detail interactive flow (coord#634/web#587, hardened by web#609/#612/#614 and UI-Bridge-instrumented by web#617). ci-bot is a superuser so the authed page renders. Two route stubs make the page deterministic without live coord: GET /api/v1/digital-twin/subspaces (the matrix probe, restricted:false) sets the cells' credibility/status, and GET /api/v1/digital-twin/subspace/schema/raw returns a DriftVerdict so the Schema drawer's 'Show raw data' toggle (gated on isSnapshot && verdict) renders. Assertions target the web#617 useUIElement ids (digital-twin-tab-*, digital-twin-cell-*, digital-twin-show-raw), which resolve against the UI-Bridge registry the matcher reads; the page titles ('Digital Twin', 'Ask the Twin') resolve via the wrapper's content-discovery pass (provider.tsx contentDiscovery enabled). STRUCTURE NOTE: spec-ci's structural lane evaluates every state against the INITIAL render, so only initially-present states are declared (shell + matrix). The cell -> drawer -> raw-verdict interaction is a transition with activateStates:[] whose waitAfter (the show-raw toggle appearing) is the pass condition. CI runs with NO connected runner, so Ask-the-Twin and the UI Bridge tab show their 'Connect a runner' empty states — their runner-only input ids (ask-the-twin-*, ui-bridge-*) do NOT mount and are NOT asserted; the cell-drawer flow needs no runner. The drawer is a modal Dialog, so a tab-switch transition is intentionally omitted (it would either be blocked by the open modal or unmount the matrix the drawer transition needs). Read-only; structural.

states.name: Explorer shell + tabs
states.description: The /digital-twin chrome that renders on the default Completeness tab: the page title, the three tab triggers (Completeness / Delivery / UI Bridge — registered via web#617 useUIElement ids), and the 'Ask the Twin' section heading (which renders in both the runner and no-runner branches — in CI there is no runner, so its input/submit controls do not mount and are not asserted). Identifies the page mounted, authenticated, not redirected.

assertions.description: h1 'Digital Twin' — the page title proving the section mounted (authed, not redirected). Resolves via content-discovery.
'Ask the Twin' section heading (h2; renders in both the runner and no-runner branches). Resolves via content-discovery.

states.name: Completeness matrix cells
states.description: The sub-space cells the matrix renders from the 25-row manifest taxonomy (the matrix denominator), joined with the stubbed GET /subspaces probe for live status. Each cell is registered with a web#617 useUIElement id digital-twin-cell-<manifestId>; this state asserts a representative set across status classes: schema (snapshot, implemented — the drawer-transition target), infra (snapshot, implemented), deps (snapshot, the 'blind' class). settleUntilPresent waits for digital-twin-cell-schema to register before the matcher snapshots, dodging the probe/render load race.

assertions.description: Schema sub-space cell (id digital-twin-cell-schema) — the drawer-transition target.
Deps sub-space cell (id digital-twin-cell-deps) — the 'blind' status class.

transitions.name: Open the Schema sub-space drawer (cell click)
transitions.description: Behavioral READ: click the Schema sub-space cell (id digital-twin-cell-schema). The detail Dialog opens; because schema is query_kind 'snapshot' and the stubbed GET /subspace/schema/raw returns a verdict, the 'Show raw data' toggle (id digital-twin-show-raw, gated on isSnapshot && verdict) renders. The waitAfter on that toggle appearing is the pass condition — proving the cell -> drawer -> raw-verdict flow works end-to-end. activateStates is empty (the structural lane only models the initial render), so this transition passes iff the click executes without error AND show-raw appears within the timeout. Needs no connected runner (the matrix + drawer are coord-backed and stubbed here).
